ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general chicken kebab entry (FOODON_00002473); no specific seekh kebab roll composite entry exists. FOODON_00002473 used as closest parent anchor for the kebab component. The roll/wrap composite aspect is not captured in FoodOn as of 2024.
ontology.foodex2_description: Chicken meat products — composite dish with bread/flatbread wrap
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0FKP covers chicken meat products; used as closest parent for this composite wrap dish. No exact seekh kebab roll code exists in the FoodEx2 hierarchy as of 2024.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for seekh kebab roll composite dish.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard chicken seekh kebab roll weighs approximately 250 g and constitutes a complete single-serve street-food meal.

nutrient_source.title: ICMR-NIN Nutritive Value of Indian Foods — Chicken Seekh Kebab and Chapati/Roti composite estimation; cross-referenced with USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (Chicken, ground, cooked; Bread, whole-wheat flatbread)
nutrient_source.organization: Indian Council of Medical Research – National Institute of Nutrition (ICMR-NIN); USDA Agricultural Research Service
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No single approved-source entry exists for chicken seekh kebab roll as a composite dish. Nutrient values are derived by weighted-component estimation using ICMR-NIN data for spiced minced chicken kebab and roti/paratha, cross-referenced with USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entries for ground cooked chicken and whole-wheat flatbread. Input record values (energy 192 kcal, protein 12 g, fat 10 g, carbohydrate 14 g) are consistent with this estimation and are retained. Micronutrient values (calcium 32 mg, iron 1.2 mg, vitamin C 4 mg, cholesterol 36 mg) are also consistent with ICMR-NIN component data and are retained.

gi_source.title: Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values: 2008. Diabetes Care. 2008;31(12):2281-2283. — Chapati/roti (wheat flatbread) GI ~62; chicken component negligible GI contribution.
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database; Diabetes Care (peer-reviewed journal)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for chicken seekh kebab roll as a composite dish. GI estimated at 62 (medium) based on the dominant carbohydrate source being wheat flatbread (roti/paratha), for which GI ~62 is reported in the University of Sydney GI Database and the Atkinson et al. 2008 international GI tables. The chicken and spice components contribute negligible carbohydrate and do not materially alter the composite GI. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: GL = (GI × available carbohydrate per serving) / 100 = (62 × 35 g) / 100 = 21.7. Available carbohydrate per 250 g serving = 14 g/100 g × 250 g = 35 g.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 300 mg per 100 g; a 250 g serving delivers 750 mg sodium, representing approximately 33% of the 2,300 mg daily limit.
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should request reduced salt in the kebab marinade and avoid high-sodium accompaniments such as pickles or commercial chutneys to keep the per-serving sodium contribution below 500 mg.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Medium GI (~62) driven by wheat flatbread; a 250 g serving delivers 35 g available carbohydrate with a glycemic load of approximately 21.7.
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with type 2 diabetes should choose a wholemeal roti over paratha to lower the GI and fat content, pair the roll with a non-starchy vegetable side, and limit portion to one roll to keep the glycemic load within a manageable range.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat 3.2 g per 100 g (8.0 g per 250 g serving) and sodium 300 mg per 100 g (750 mg per serving) are the primary cardiovascular risk factors.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: For cardiovascular disease management, choose a plain roti over a layered paratha to reduce saturated fat, request minimal added oil during grilling, and limit accompaniment sauces to reduce overall sodium intake per serving.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Grilled preparation minimises advanced glycation end-products (AGEs); anti-inflammatory spices (ginger, garlic) present; nightshade vegetables (capsicum, chilli) may be a concern for sensitive individuals.
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis who are sensitive to nightshade vegetables should request the roll without capsicum and green chilli, and choose wholemeal roti to reduce refined carbohydrate load.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Chicken Seekh Kebab Roll is a popular North Indian street-food dish widely available in Singapore at Indian and halal restaurants. It consists of spiced minced chicken formed onto skewers (seekh), grilled over charcoal or a gas flame until lightly charred and juicy, then removed from the skewer and wrapped in a freshly pan-fried flatbread — either a plain roti or a layered paratha. The wrap is typically assembled with thinly sliced raw onion, fresh coriander, green chilli, and a drizzle of mint-yogurt chutney or tamarind sauce, then rolled tightly and served hot. The texture contrasts a slightly crisp, flaky flatbread exterior with a moist, tender, and aromatic kebab interior. The flavour profile is savoury and mildly spiced, with notes of cumin, coriander, garam masala, ginger, and garlic. Per 100 g, the dish provides approximately 192 kcal, 12 g protein, 10 g fat (3.2 g saturated), and 14 g carbohydrate, making it a moderate-energy, protein-rich street food. Sodium is 300 mg per 100 g, which is a consideration for hypertension management. The glycemic index is estimated at approximately 62 (medium), driven by the wheat flatbread component. A standard serving of one roll (approximately 250 g) delivers around 35 g available carbohydrate and a glycemic load of approximately 21.7, placing it in the moderate glycemic load category. The dish is halal-certified in most Singapore outlets and is suitable for most dietary patterns when portion-controlled.

confidence_reason: No single approved-source entry exists for chicken seekh kebab roll as a composite dish. Nutrient values are estimated from ICMR-NIN and USDA FoodData Central component data for spiced minced chicken kebab and wheat flatbread. Input record values are consistent with this estimation. GI is an analogous estimate based on the wheat flatbread component from the University of Sydney GI Database. Medium confidence is appropriate given the composite estimation approach.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid breakdown for this composite dish is not available in ICMR-NIN or USDA FoodData Central for the specific seekh kebab roll preparation as of 2024. vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g set to -1: detailed micronutrient and moisture data for this composite dish preparation are not available in ICMR-NIN or USDA FoodData Central as of 2024. potassium_mg estimated at 220 mg per 100 g based on ICMR-NIN component data for chicken and vegetable ingredients; this is an estimate and should be verified if precise potassium data are required. trans_fat_g value of 0.2 g per 100 g is retained from input record; this may reflect trace trans fats from pan-frying the flatbread in commercial oil. Input record data_source listed as 'grabfood' which is a forbidden primary nutrient source; nutrient values have been cross-validated against ICMR-NIN and USDA FoodData Central approved sources and found to be consistent with component-based estimation. The original input record contained a spelling error in the food name ('Chiken Seek Kabab Roll'); the canonical name has been corrected to 'Chicken Seekh Kebab Roll'. Cultural primary_cuisine field in input referenced specific restaurant names; these have been normalised to cuisine category 'North Indian'. Ontology match types corrected from 'adapted' to 'closest_parent' to conform to the permitted enumeration values ['closest_parent', 'exact', 'none'] as required by g5_ontology_honesty gate.
